reasoning: The control 'Basic Security for Smaller NBFCs' focuses on fundamental security practices such as access control, network security, and data protection, which map well to Kubernetes security domains. RBAC controls ensure least privilege and prevent excessive permissions, similar to IAM roles and policies. Network security is enforced through NetworkPolicies to restrict ingress traffic, akin to cloud security groups. Secrets management aligns with ensuring encryption at rest, similar to cloud KMS or Secrets Manager. These mappings are directly applicable to Kubernetes environments.
title: Basic Security for Smaller NBFCs
